title: Myrcia subverticillaris
intro: Myrcia subverticillaris, also known as Brazilian peppertree or guava, is a small to medium-sized tree or shrub native to South America, belonging to the family Myrtaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in the treatment of various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Myrcia subverticillaris include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, such as quercetin, kaempferol, and geraniol, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, or used in the form of capsules or extracts, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
